step2_rag_chunks: When a craving hits, delaying the response by even 5 to 10 minutes is often enough for it to pass. Tell yourself you will smoke in 10 minutes if you still want to â€” and then do something else for those 10 minutes. Most of the time the urge has weakened significantly by the time the 10 minutes are up.
Cravings for tobacco are temporary and usually last between 5 and 10 minutes. When a craving occurs, delay smoking and focus on another activity until the urge passes.
Stress is a common trigger for smoking. Relaxation techniques such as deep breathing, meditation, or short walks can help manage stress without using tobacco.

Do NOT summarize what was discussed. Just acknowledge their readiness warmly and wish them well. Example: 'That sounds like a solid plan — take it one step at a time.' One sentence only. Nothing more.

Therapist: Great! Finally, I want to let you know about some available resources that can help you quit. There are hotlines, support groups, and online resources that can provide you with additional support and guidance. Would you like me to give you some information about those?
Patient: Yeah, that would be great. Thanks.
Patient: Yeah, that would be great. Thanks.
Therapist:
step7_response: It's wonderful that you're taking proactive steps towards your well-being – I wish you all the best in your journey forward.
